transcript.clean_text: foreign stage live as the general Italia reaches its conclusion on gcn plus where you can also find highlights and on-demand replays Mark Cavendish was lining up a day after announcing his retirement Bruno Mar looking to keep the pink jersey but it will be an uphill task for him Timo Pino and with the general Italia set to go into the mighty mountains a minute and eight seconds the Frenchman's lead Army High realistically knowing that it would be a tough uphill ask once the race left the shores of Lake Garda heading up just over 203 kilometers to the top of Monte bondoni the trouble was that wasn't the only time of the day several of them in fact classified climbing tough climbing more meters of climbing than any other stage of this Italia adding up to well over five and a half thousand since 2006 and Ivan basso's win a summit finished famous too for the exploits of Charlie Gould back in the 1950s the first hour was ridden at 52 kilometers per hour and it took a while to see the Breakaway formed confusion in the various tunnels alongside the lake once it was formed it was a big one and Ben Healy was there taking the maximum points at the top of the first classified climb he'd be on his way to moving into the king of the mountains Jersey and Astana Kazakhstan were on their way to trying to surprise everybody bronsky and scaroni setting off but yombo visma behind with The Gap at five minutes to the mayadosa group laying their fist on the table and deciding that the GC battle was finally going to be unpaused was a danger up the road at just four minutes and 30 in the GC Jack Hague and Patrick Conrad were there as well when they got on to the final climb though the break started to break up and yombo visma continued to put the Pace on Zana was there for Eddie Dunbar behind several other satellite Riders too including Jack Hague Diego Lisi be looking to do that job and the Peloton behind or what was left of it was getting closer once jumbo vizma finished it was up to u80 Emirates first Formula then Vine and with 10ks to go the Malia also was in trouble you carthy was struggling as well several Riders finding that the pace was too much once J Vine was done Almeida was on the attack starting the day in fourth position but only 22 seconds behind the Magnetic North Garrett Thomas Primos roglitz was in there and still had sepkus and he'd need him six kilometers to go al-meda set off on the attack Dunbar the Survivor the best of the rest Thomas jumped across to him with four and a half K's to go and tried to distance the Portuguese he wouldn't manage that but he would really put roglitch in trouble half a minute put into him at one stage roglitch thanking his lucky stars and sepkus for still being in touch he'd get closer towards the end and Thomas should be riding into the final 500 meters Joel maida the best young Rider Garrett Thomas the man looking to become the oldest ever winner of the race both of them in tandem working well Almeida taking his first ever Grant to a stage win only the third Portuguese Giro win down the years Thomas finishing with him and moving back into the maliarosa and 25 seconds lost by Primos rogrich and Eddie Dunbar he however moving up into fifth place in the GC not great day for Caruso Leonard Candler losing a bit of time all the glory to Almeida and garant Thomas putting the G in Judo having an 18 second lead in The GC Almeida Thomas and roglich Dunbar and cuss in the top five as well Garrett Thomas had lost a teammate throughout the day with Pavel silvikov having to climb off but he's looking better by the date the Welshman 36 will turn 37 in a couple of days he leads by 18 seconds from Almeida 29 in front of roglitch and then it's 250 back to Fourth Place Caruso and 303 to Dunbar who's moved up to fifth returning to the shoulders of Welshman Garrett Thomas inyos Grenadier is in the lead but there are some big stages still to go Ben Healy on a good day for Irish cycling with Dunbar under the top five moves into the king of the mountains Jersey he now has a 20-point lead but no king of the mountains points to be played out on stage 17 should be one for the sprinters or maybe those who can hold off weaker Sprint teams from the Breakaway it's another long day almost 200 kilometers going downhill from the mountains and to The Plains of the Veneto finishing in cowardly intermediate Sprint at Lido yes and that finish with no lumps and bumps really after 197 kilometers the jedo d'Italia continues vegetalia is in play on gcn plus we can watch it live and On Demand each each day
